comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Current root-cause hypothesis: Ralph needs a bounded synchronous handoff, not either extreme. Evidence in pi-mono: AgentSession.prompt awaits extension slash commands synchronously via _tryExecuteExtensionCommand(), while pi.sendUserMessage() is fire-and-forget and only emits errors asynchronously. If /ralph start awaits the entire runLoop, the command never releases the interactive turn; if Ralph is fully detached before ctx.newSession/switchSession/sendFollowUp complete, those session-control calls run outside the command lifecycle and the UI/session binding is lost. The safe shape is likely: stay synchronous through controller->child session handoff and first child follow-up submission, then return control and let later loop progress be event-driven.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Implemented candidate fix after reproducing the missing-widget root cause in tests. Root cause: on child session creation, interactive-mode resets extension UI and rebinds extensions before Ralph persists child ownership. In a fresh tau runtime, currentLoopRef is empty, so session_start -> updateUI resolved no loop and cleared the widget immediately. Fix: Ralph.resolveLoopForUi now falls back to the sole active loop in the cwd when there is no current-loop memory and no session ownership match yet. Added regression coverage in ralph-core-service.test.ts for fresh-runtime UI recovery, while retaining the command-bound session/file and execution-profile fixes.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Follow-up root cause after the widget fix: the active child session was correct, but Ralph prompt delivery still came from the pre-handoff command runtime. In tau, the ManagedRuntime is process-scoped, but each pi session rebind creates a fresh extension runtime/API object. After ctx.newSession()/switchSession, command-side pi.sendUserMessage() can target a stale runtime even while ownership.child and the visible session file already match. Fix: register per-session prompt dispatchers on session_start/session_switch/session_fork, unregister on session_shutdown, and have RalphCommandBoundary.sendFollowUp route through the dispatcher for the tracked current session file. Keep a same-session fallback only when the command context still points at the target session. Added regression coverage proving prompts dispatch through the active child-session runtime after newSession.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: New root cause for the post-handoff stall: continuation state lived in the shared loop file, but the wait-for-agent_end latch lived inside the Ralph service instance that started /ralph start. Session handoff creates a fresh tau runtime per session rebind, so child-session tools/events (ralph_continue + agent_end) ran in a different Ralph service instance and never resolved the controller runtime's waiting latch. Result: pendingDecision=continue persisted, ownership.child stayed on the finished iteration session, and no next child session was created. Fix: move iteration signal delivery onto a process-global bridge keyed by iteration session file, with queued-signal support so agent_end/session_shutdown can wake waits across fresh runtimes. Added regression coverage that starts runLoop in one runtime and delivers ralph_continue + agent_end from a fresh child runtime, proving iteration 2 starts.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: New pi update added stale context enforcement. Live repro: /ralph start <name> fails with Extension command:ralph error: This extension ctx is stale after session replacement or reload. Root investigation focus: Ralph still uses the command ctx after ctx.newSession()/switchSession instead of doing replacement-session work through withSession.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Root cause confirmed against the pi session lifecycle update: Ralph command boundary continued to use the command ctx in cleanup/profile/UI paths after ctx.newSession replaced the session. Added regression coverage with a stale-after-newSession command ctx; Ralph now binds the replacement ctx from withSession, applies execution profiles through that ctx, routes follow-up dispatch away from stale command ctx/pi, and treats stale ctx during post-handoff cleanup as an expected replaced-session condition. Gate passes.

comments.author: backlog
comments.text: Live /ralph resume xyz now reports 'Creating Ralph iteration session was cancelled.' New root hypothesis: resume first switches back to the controller session, making the original command ctx stale, then RalphCommandBoundary.newSession still calls the original ctx instead of the active replacement ctx. Need regression for resume-from-non-controller: switchSession with withSession must rebind future session-control calls before creating the iteration session.
